source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/04_2G & 3G (A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW)/▶ CE MDR/231013 컨설팅 추가 리뷰(CER 외)/아비즈 MDR TCF_231013/A-TCF-5-157 Clinical Evaluation/A-TCF-5-117 Clinical Evaluation Plan (rev.1).docx

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical evaluation plan for A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detectors under MDR clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation planning, GSPR clinical evidence identification, benefit-risk assessment, equivalence, literature search, PMS/PMCF, and standards mapping for MDR technical documentation.
keyPoints: The plan states it is documented according to Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The device is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children, except infants and pregnant women.
Clinical benefits are reduction in patient dose with high image quality and fast image acquisition time.
Clinical outcome parameters include DQE, MTF, recovery time, and image acquisition time.
The document states there is no common specification for the device.
The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every two years.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: Our device (A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is designed by modifications of a device already marketed by us(H&abyz Co., Ltd.).

entities.name: Carestream Health
entities.evidence: DRX-1 of Carestream Health (CE marked by NB 0086)

entities.name: Canon
entities.evidence: CXDI-70C of Canon (CE marked by NB 0197)

concepts.name: Clinical evaluation under MDR Annex XIV Part A
concepts.evidence: This clinical evaluation plan is documented according to the Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

concepts.name: Demonstration of equivalence
concepts.evidence: A clinical evaluation may be based on clinical data relating to a device for which equivalence to the device in question can be demonstrated.

concepts.name: General Safety and Performance Requirements
concepts.evidence: The general safety and performance requirements that require support from relevant clinical data are identified.

concepts.name: Post-market clinical follow-up
concepts.evidence: The PMCF will be conducted as a part of the PMS activities.

concepts.name: Post-market surveillance
concepts.evidence: According to the PMS plan (A-PMS-5-001 Post-market Surveillance Plan), we will collect and utilize available information such as incident documentation, feedback and customer complaints, compile list of trend depending on collected data, scientific literature, and recall data.

concepts.name: Detective Quantum Efficiency
concepts.evidence: Relevant clinical outcome parameters Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE)

concepts.name: Modulation Transfer Function
concepts.evidence: Relevant clinical outcome parameters Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) Modulation Transfer Function (MTF)

evidence.claim: The document is a clinical evaluation plan for a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector clinical evaluation plan

evidence.claim: The product models covered are A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Model name: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: Revision 1 is described as a response to first review by 3EC (NB).
evidence.evidenceText: 1 날짜 Response to 1st review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: The initial release date for revision 0 is December 01, 2022.
evidence.evidenceText: 0 December 01, 2022 Initial release

evidence.claim: The plan is documented according to Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical evaluation plan is documented according to the Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector (model: A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The intended target groups are adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Adult and children except infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector target population

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: Clinical benefit 1 is reduction in patient dose and high quality image.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 1 – Reduction in patient dose and high quality image.

evidence.claim: Clinical benefit 2 is fast image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 2 – Fast image acquisition time

evidence.claim: DQE and MTF are relevant clinical outcome parameters for reduction in patient dose and high quality image.
evidence.evidenceText: Relevant clinical outcome parameters Detective Quantum Efficiency (DQE) Modulation Transfer Function (MTF)

evidence.claim: Recovery time and image acquisition time are relevant clinical outcome parameters for fast image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Relevant clinical outcome parameters Recovery time Image acquisition time

evidence.claim: Performance objectives are measured and tested in accordance with EN 62220-1-1.
evidence.evidenceText: The relevant clinical outcome parameters of these intended clinical benefits lead to performance requirements for the Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector, which will be measured and tested in accordance with EN 62220-1-1.

evidence.claim: The device complies with biocompatibility requirements according to EN ISO 10993-1 series.
evidence.evidenceText: The device complies with the biocompatibility requirements according to EN ISO 10993-1 series (A-TCF-5-144 Biocompatibility Assessment Report).

evidence.claim: The device complies with EN 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic compatibility.
evidence.evidenceText: The device complies with Electromagnetic Compatibility of medical electrical equipment according to EN 60601-1-2 (A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports).

evidence.claim: The device complies with EN 60601-1 for basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: The device complies with the basic safety and essential performance of medical electrical equipment according to EN 60601-1 (A-TCF-5-156 Test Reports).

evidence.claim: The literature study uses PubMed, European Radiology, Medical Physics, FDA Recall, MFDS recall sources, and MHRA alerts and recalls.
evidence.evidenceText: Scientific literature databases use PubMed, European Radiology and Medical Physics, and Internet searches use FDA Recall, MFDS Product recall and Suspension of Sales and MHRA Alerts and Recalls for Drugs and Medical Devices to collect relevant data.

evidence.claim: Equivalence evaluation should comply with MDCG 2020-5.
evidence.evidenceText: The equivalence evaluation should comply with MDCG 2020-5 clinical evaluation – equivalence, and analyze data published in the scientific literature to consider equivalence based on proper scientific justification.

evidence.claim: The document states the device is not subject to clinical investigation.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device is not subject to Clinical investigation. Please refer to section 9 of this documentation.

evidence.claim: The device contacts intact skin surfaces only.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device contacts intact skin surfaces only.

evidence.claim: There is no common specification for the device.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no common specification for the device.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every two years.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every two years.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date for revision 1 is not provided; the source shows the Korean placeholder '날짜' for the revision 1 date and signatures.
Source metadata authoritySignal is FDA, but the document content is primarily CE MDR/Regulation (EU) 2017/745; FDA appears only as a recall search source.
Revision 0 initial release date is present, but it should not be treated as the document date for revision 1.
Some Korean and English duplicate sections are present; extracted claims use the English text where available.
